title: Planet Labs Q4 2026 slides: first profitable year, backlog surges 79%
text:
Planet Labs achieved a historic milestone by reaching its first full year of non-GAAP profitability in fiscal 2026. The company reported Q4 2026 Revenue of $86.8 million, exceeding analyst expectations of $77.81 million by 11.55%. Full-Year 2026 Revenue was $307.7 million, representing 26% year-over-year growth. The backlog surged 79% year-over-year to $900 million. Adjusted EBITDA was $15.5 million positive (versus $11 million loss in prior year). Free Cash Flow was $52.9 million (compared to negative $64 million previously). Cash position was $640.1 million in cash, equivalents, and short-term investments.

Non-GAAP gross margin stood at 59% for the full year, maintaining strong profitability discipline. The company demonstrated steady acceleration with revenue growing from $131 million in fiscal 2022 to $308 million in 2026, achieving a 24% compound annual growth rate.

Major contract wins included a multi-year agreement with Sweden's Armed Forces and selection as a prime contractor with the U.S. Missile Defense Agency. Government and defense segments showed the strongest momentum, expanding over 50% year-over-year.

For fiscal 2027, Planet projects revenue of $415-440 million (35-43% growth), with adjusted EBITDA expected near breakeven to $10 million as the company invests in satellite deployment and AI solution development.
